---
status: draft
shortDescription: "Java implicitly produces implementations of any methods a class inherits from the interfaces it implements"
concepts:
  - Class
  - Method
  - Inheritance
related:
# - id: LiteralNoExpression
#   relation: Special case
inOtherPl:
# - pl: JavaScript
#   id: ImplicitInterfaceImplementation
# - pl: Python
#   id: ImplicitInterfaceImplementation
spec:
# - id: jls13
#   sections: ["11.8"]
plVersion:
# from: jls8
# to: jls14
---
When we state that a class "implements" an interface, this means that Java will automatically generate implementations of all the methods declared in that interface.

frontmatter.shortDescription: Java implicitly produces implementations of any methods a class inherits from the interfaces it implements
